quick_facts.label: What this node represents
quick_facts.value: A Bachelor of Arts (BA) earned outside India is an undergraduate degree whose exact title, length, subject options, credit rules, and admissions criteria are set by the foreign university and destination system; it is not a single standardized Indian qualification.

quick_facts.label: Academic eligibility for Indian school-leavers
quick_facts.value: Requirements are institution-specific. For example, Oxford lists CBSE Year XII and CISCE ISC as accepted qualifications with course-dependent high grades, while listing Indian state-board examinations and NIOS Year XII as not accepted for its undergraduate entry route.

quick_facts.label: IELTS relevance
quick_facts.value: IELTS Academic is intended for overseas higher education, but the accepted test format, minimum band scores, exemptions, and score-submission deadline must be checked with each university. Oxford, for example, recommends IELTS Academic and specifies an overall 7.5 with at least 7.0 in every component for its undergraduate requirement.

quick_facts.label: SAT relevance
quick_facts.value: SAT scores can contribute to overseas undergraduate admission where a university accepts or requires them, but policies differ materially by institution. College Board states that more than 4,000 institutions in over 85 countries accept SAT and/or AP for admission, credit, placement, or a combination; applicants must still follow the policy of each target university.

quick_facts.label: Duration
quick_facts.value: There is no universal BA duration abroad. As dated examples, Oxford lists its BA in Human Sciences as a three-year course, while UBC’s BA framework requires at least 120 eligible credits rather than describing a single universal duration.

sections.heading: Eligibility: secondary-school achievement and English readiness
paragraphs: An Indian applicant should begin with the target university’s country-specific qualification page and the chosen BA course’s subject requirements. Overseas institutions do not apply one common conversion for Class XII results. Oxford’s published undergraduate guidance, for instance, sets different CBSE/CISCE grade profiles according to the A-level-equivalent demand of the course and may require particular subjects relevant to the degree.

English-language evidence is also university-specific. IELTS Academic is widely used for study abroad, but an applicant should not assume that an IELTS result is always required or that one band score works everywhere: Oxford allows certain English-medium education exemptions and otherwise sets its own test and component-score requirements.
bullets: Check whether the university accepts the applicant’s board and qualification, not merely the aggregate Class XII percentage.
Check course-specific prerequisites; arts and humanities programmes may require subjects, written work, admissions tests, interviews, or portfolios depending on the course.
Confirm whether IELTS Academic, another accepted test, or an exemption applies, and verify the required score and validity/submission rules directly with the university.

sections.heading: Duration: determined by country and programme design
paragraphs: A foreign BA should be evaluated programme by programme rather than assumed to take a fixed number of years. A current UK illustration is Oxford’s BA in Human Sciences, which is listed as three years. Credit-based North American programmes can be structured differently; UBC’s BA requires a minimum of 120 BA-eligible credits.
Before applying, confirm the normal study load, compulsory placement/year-abroad elements, maximum time allowed, and whether transfer or advanced standing could change the individual completion plan.
bullets: Illustrative three-year model: Oxford BA in Human Sciences.
Illustrative credit model: UBC BA requires at least 120 eligible credits.
The examples are not a rule for every foreign BA.

sections.heading: Curriculum: major study combined with breadth and skills requirements
paragraphs: BA curricula commonly combine depth in a chosen major with broader study and academic-skills requirements, but the balance is set locally. UBC’s current BA rules are a useful official example: students entering in 2024/25 or later need at least 120 eligible credits, including writing and research, breadth study, a major of at least 42 credits, and upper-level and Arts-credit minimums.

Accordingly, applicants should compare course catalogues rather than rely only on a degree title. A BA called English, History, Politics, Psychology, Economics, Languages, Philosophy, or an interdisciplinary subject can have very different compulsory modules and assessment formats.
bullets: Review the required major, minor/elective options, research and writing requirements, and any language, quantitative, laboratory, internship, or study-abroad components.
Confirm whether a major is declared on entry, selected after the first year, or subject to a later competitive application.

sections.heading: Admission: use SAT and IELTS only as required by the target university
paragraphs: SAT is an admissions credential, not a substitute for school qualifications, transcripts, course prerequisites, essays, recommendations, portfolios, or immigration documentation where those are required. College Board reports broad international recognition, but each university decides whether SAT scores are required, optional, considered for scholarships, or not used.

Published institutional policies demonstrate why applicants must verify the exact rule. Harvard currently says first-year international applicants normally must meet its standardized-testing requirement with the SAT or ACT, with limited alternatives when access is not feasible; it also says IELTS cannot satisfy that standardized-testing requirement, although it may be submitted for review. Conversely, Oxford’s published undergraduate pathway emphasizes accepted school qualifications, course-specific conditions, and English-language evidence where applicable.

Applicants should build an application calendar around the destination and intake year, and should treat all deadlines and testing policies as changeable. For example, Oxford states that applications for 2027 entry or deferred 2028 entry are due on 15 October 2026; that date is an Oxford-specific example, not a general overseas-BA deadline.
bullets: Read the course page and the international-admissions page before registering for SAT or IELTS.
Send only the test type and score report requested by the university; for English proof, confirm whether IELTS Academic is required or an exemption applies.
Track separate deadlines for the university application, testing, financial-aid or scholarship forms, and student-visa process.

sections.heading: Costs: compare tuition and living expenses using the entry-year figures
paragraphs: Foreign-BA costs vary sharply by university, country, course, fee status, exchange rate, accommodation choice, and annual fee changes. Applicants should build a full budget that separates tuition from living costs, insurance, travel, visa charges, books, and other course-specific expenses.

As dated 2026-entry context only, Oxford publishes overseas undergraduate tuition ranging from £37,380 to £62,820 for 2026/27 depending on programme. For its Human Sciences BA, Oxford lists overseas annual course fees of £43,600 for 2026 entry and estimates living costs for 2026/27 at £1,405–£2,105 per month while in Oxford. Oxford also warns that fees usually increase annually; these figures should therefore not be used as a quote for another university, course, or intake.
bullets: Use the university’s published international/overseas fee classification, not domestic fee figures.
Check whether tuition is charged annually and whether the university forecasts annual increases.
Budget living costs separately from tuition and investigate scholarships, bursaries, institutional aid, and external funding before accepting an offer.
